title: What is a cash on delivery service?

parentDocument.title: What is a cash on delivery service?
parentDocument.content:
What is a cash on delivery service?
Cash on delivery (COD)is a payment method used in e-commerce and mail orders where payment for goods is made at the time of delivery rather than in advance. In a COD transaction, the buyer makes payment in cash or by check or credit card upon receiving the goods.
COD services are typically offered bycourieror delivery companies and are popular in regions where online payment options are limited or not trusted. COD services provide customers with an added level of security as they only have to pay when they receive the goods, rather than in advance. This can be particularly useful for customers who are concerned about online fraud or who do not have access to credit cards.
COD services are also useful for businesses that want to reach customers who are not comfortable with online payments. However, COD services can be costly for businesses as the courier or delivery company usually charges a fee for the service. Additionally, there is a risk that the customer may refuse to accept the delivery or not have enough cash to pay, which can result in additional costs and delays for the seller.

title: What is a delivery service?

parentDocument.title: What is a delivery service?
parentDocument.content:
What is a delivery service?
Adelivery serviceprovider is a business that specializes in transporting goods or packages from one location to another. Delivery services can be used by individuals, small businesses, and large corporations to transport items such as food, groceries, retail products, documents, and other goods.
Delivery services can operate on a local, regional, or national level and may offer a variety of delivery options, including same-day delivery, next-day delivery, and standard delivery. They may also provide tracking services so that customers can monitor the progress of their deliveries.
Delivery services typically use various modes of transportation, including cars, vans, trucks, and bicycles, depending on the distance and size of the delivery. Some delivery services may also use drones or other types of unmanned aerial vehicles to transport small items.
Unlike courier services, which may focus on delivering documents and small packages, delivery services tend to focus on perishables items like foods, and groceries, or larger items such as furniture, appliances, and groceries. Additionally, delivery services may offer additional services such as the assembly or installation of products for an additional fee.

title: What is a courier service?

parentDocument.title: What is a courier service?
parentDocument.content:
What is a courier service?
Acourier serviceprovider is a company or organization that specializes in delivering parcels, documents, and other types of shipments from one location to another. Courier services provide fast and reliable delivery options for businesses and individuals who need to send items quickly and securely.
Courier service providers can operate on a local, national, or international level, depending on the needs of their customers. They typically offer a range of delivery options, including same-day delivery, next-day delivery, and standard delivery, and may also providetrackingservices so that customers can monitor the progress of their shipments.
Courier services may use a variety of transportation methods, including cars, vans, trucks, motorcycles, bicycles, or even drones, depending on the distance, urgency, and size of the shipment. They may also offer specialized services, such as secure document delivery, temperature-controlled shipping, and fragile item handling.

title: Volumetric Weight

parentDocument.title: Volumetric Weight
parentDocument.content:
Understanding Volumetric Weight and How It Affects Your Shipping Costs
When shipping a package, especially through courier services, you may come across a term called volumetric weight. It is an important concept that can directly impact the shipping costs of your package. This guide will explain what volumetric weight is, how it’s calculated, and why it matters.
What is Volumetric Weight?
Volumetric weight, also known asdimensional weight, is a method used by courier services to calculate shipping costs based on the size (volume) of your package rather than its actual weight. This concept is particularly important for lightweight packages that take up significant space in delivery vehicles.
For example, a box of feathers might weigh very little but occupy a lot of space.
To ensure fair pricing, courier companies use volumetric weight to account for the space taken up by the package.
Why Does Volumetric Weight Matter?
Shipping companies have limited space in their delivery vehicles and airplanes. By calculating volumetric weight, they can optimize space utilization and ensure that pricing reflects not just the actual weight of the package but also the space it occupies. This ensures that both lightweight and heavy items are priced appropriately.
For shippers, understanding volumetric weight is crucial to avoid unexpected shipping costs. A package with large dimensions but light weight could end up costing more than a smaller, heavier package.
How to Calculate Volumetric Weight?
The formula for volumetric weight may vary slightly among courier companies, but the most commonly used formula is:
Volumetric Weight (kg) = (Length x Width x Height) / Volumetric Factor
Length,Width, andHeightare measured in centimeters (cm).
TheVolumetric Factoris a constant set by the courier service. For most couriers, the standard factor is5,000or6,000.
Let’s say your package dimensions are as follows:
Using the formula with a volumetric factor of 5,000:Volumetric Weight = (50 x 30 x 20) / 5,000Volumetric Weight = 30 kg
If theactual weightof the package is only 10 kg, the courier will charge you based on thehigher weight, which is the volumetric weight (30 kg).
Volumetric Weight vs. Actual Weight
Shipping costs are usually determined based on whichever is higher between:
Actual Weight(the weight of the package on a scale).
Volumetric Weight(calculated based on the dimensions of the package).
Couriers charge based on the higher of the two to ensure fair pricing for space and weight.
Tips to Optimize Your Shipping Costs
Pack Efficiently:Use appropriate-sized boxes that fit your items snugly. Avoid using overly large boxes for small items.
Compress Items:If possible, compress lightweight items to reduce the package dimensions.
Compare Shipping Options:Different couriers may use different volumetric factors. Compare their pricing to get the best deal.
Use DelyvaNow:Platforms like DelyvaNow allow you to compare multiple courier services in one place, so you can find the most cost-effective option based on your package’s dimensions and weight.
Why Volumetric Weight is Important for E-Commerce Businesses
For online sellers, understanding volumetric weight is critical to avoid losses on shipping. If shipping fees are not calculated correctly, businesses may undercharge customers, leading to higher expenses. E-commerce platforms and shipping software often include tools to calculate volumetric weight to ensure accurate pricing.
Volumetric weight ensures fair shipping pricing by taking both weight and size into account. By understanding and calculating volumetric weight, you can avoid unexpected costs and ship your packages more efficiently.
If you’re using DelyvaNow, our platform automatically calculates volumetric weight for you when booking a shipment, saving you time and effort.
Have more questions about volumetric weight or shipping? Contact us, and we’ll be happy to assist!

title: Packaging and courier bags

parentDocument.title: Packaging and courier bags
parentDocument.content:
Packaging and courier bags
To ensure your package delivered safely and on time, please ensure you follow these packaging guidelines and procedures:
Firstly, remember to always label your package with our Consignment Note or Tracking Number. For third-party services, you must use the provided Consignment Note. i.e. Poslaju service.
Remove any markings or labels on the package that is no longer applicable.
For packages with multiple items inside, please wrap each item individually before placing them in a box.
Use adequate cushioning material (i.e.: bubble wrap, crumpled paper, etc).
Use strong tape to secure your box or parcel.
Properly seal your package to avoid exposure to foreign elements (i.e. direct sunlight, rainwater, dust, etc).
Do not use string or paper over-wrap.
For odd or irregular-shaped items, please wrap and tape all sharped edges or protrusions.
You may use any flyers or courier bags you have. Just make sure to attach the shipping label/consignment note generated by DelyvaNow.
We recommend for you to have a thermal printer to print AWB nor you can print using normal printer however recommend to insert AWB inside the transparent cover plastic to avoid AWB.

3. Fill in the sender and receiver details at1. ‘Sender Details’ & ‘2. Receiver Details’
4. Fill in parcel details and choose your package-ready date & time. Please be informed that this section is not the exact pick-up or drop-off date & time. For pick-up service, couriers will pick-up based on their pick-up schedulewithin their area.
5. Choose a preferred delivery service. Some services may have a minimum pick-up parcel requirement that has been stated on the service description.
6. Print out the consignment note provided and attach it to the parcel.
7. Wait for the courier to pick up or drop off at the nearest courier branch.

title: Which consignment note copy do I attach on parcel?

parentDocument.title: Which consignment note copy do I attach on parcel?
parentDocument.content:
Which consignment note copy do I attach on parcel?
If your consignment note layout is in A4-3 format:
Origin Copy – keep for sender’s copy
Billing Copy – give to the courier upon pick up/drop off
Receiver’s Copy – attach to the parcel
If your consignment note layout is in A5-2 format:
Billing Copy – give to the courier upon pick up/drop off
Receiver’s Copy – attach to the parcel
If your consignment note layout is in A6-1 format:
Receiver’s Copy – attach to the parcel

title: Order Packaging Guidelines

parentDocument.title: Order Packaging Guidelines
parentDocument.content:
Order Packaging Guidelines
Packing your orders properly will ensure that shoppers receive them in good condition.
How should I pack my orders?
You may need to use different packing techniques and materials, depending on the type of products to be shipped. Be sure to familiarise yourself with the different packing requirements for different types of products.
Wrap the item with bubbles wrap
Pack any accessories separately
Choose box or flyers that is bigger that the item and fill in the extra space with filler materials.
Tape the openings of the box or flyers tightly.
Special packing tips for clothes
Fold the clothes properly so that it can be fit into the flyers
Wrap the clothes with parchment paper
Pack the clothes properly and seal the flyers.
Special packing tips for high-value electronics
High-value electronics such as tablet computers, laptops, monitors and TVs are fragile and should be packed carefully to prevent damage. Follow these steps when packing high-value electronics.
Use a sturdy cardboard that is bigger than the item.
Place accessories in different plastic bag.
Use styrofoam padding to fill in the extra space.
Tape the opening of cardboard tightly.
For Electronic Devices, the items will come together with the box. So, seller has to take extra step to ensure that the parcel is well protected and the item inside is safe from damaged. Retail packaging is usually not enough to provide adequate protection during transit. Put extra layer of bubble wrap around the packaging to absorb impact.
Special packing tips for liquids
Liquids such as soap, shampoo, cream, and other beauty / personal care products may leak or spill when in transit. Follow these steps to ensure that liquid products are properly packed.
Seal the cap tightly and wrap it with bubbles wrap.
For packages with multiple items inside, please wrap each item individually before placing them in a box.
Fill in the extra space in box to put the liquid with fillers and bubble wrap.
Seal the box or flyers with tape properly.
For liquid products, secure the bottom of the box with extra tape so bottles will not fall out. The cap of the item must be secured by tape to avoid any leakage and the whole item must be covered by thick bubble wrap (at least 3 layers). Also, the gap inside the box must be filled to ensure there is no movement when courier is handling the parcel.
Seller should also use hard and sturdy box to ensure it can hold the weight of items inside.
Special packing tips for glassware/fragile item
√Do pack the item individually and separately, internal packing must be no empty space
√Do use filling material to pack each of the fragile item (Example material like Bubble Wrap, Foam Wrap, Cardboard etc) internal packing must be no empty space
√Do use strong & sturdy double-wall corrugated boxes
XDo not allow fragile item touch each other
How can I maximise protection to my parcels?
Products which are packed with minimum packaging and does not have enough protection will increase possibility of damaged parcel during delivery and lead to high return and refund cases especially for bulky items.
To prevent damage during shipment, always pack your parcels carefully with adequate protection and label the fragile items with bubbles wrap, fragile sticker, air cushion, filler and tight tape.
√Small item need to package according to the size of the box
XDo not packing using big box with a small item and gives empty space for shipment to move.
√Use a waterproof, sealable primary container.
√Pad the empty spaces of additional containers so that they don’t touch each other – the materials should also be absorbant to contain any leakage.
√Ensure the bottles are placed upright (you can use markings on the outside of the package to ensure they stay this way in transit).
√The final, outer container should be able to contain any potential leakage.
What documents do I need when I pack my order?
Before shipping your order, you will need to have the airway bill for your order.
The airway bill contains important information about the order and is a proof of consignment. Here’s an example of an airway bill with Pos Laju.
Before shipping your order:
Print 1 copy of the airway bill in preferred size.
Attach the airway bill securely to the parcel or pouch, ensuring that the barcode is clearly displayed.
Before shipping your orders. Make sure you check all the guidelines and checklist to avoid any dispute between sellers and buyers. Having a checklist is a good things to do before you ship your item. This checklist is to ensure that all action have been take before parcel head out.

title: What to do if courier does not show up?

parentDocument.title: What to do if courier does not show up?
parentDocument.content:
What to do if courier does not show up?
The usual pick-up time for courier service is from 9 am – 6 pm, anytime between that. If the courier does not show up until 6 pm, you may contact our Customer Service for us to reschedule for pick up.
Recheck if your schedule time select upon booking is out of courier cut off time. If yes, courier will arrange on the next working day.

title: What if I’m not there when courier arrives for pickup?

parentDocument.title: What if I’m not there when courier arrives for pickup?
parentDocument.content:
What if I’m not there when courier arrives for pickup?
If the courier has arrived at your location and no package is available for pickup within 5 minutes from arrival time, and you cannot be contacted or you have provided an invalid pickup address, the courier driver will continue with his route to another pickup location.
In such cases, you can inform our Customer Service to reschedule for pick up with preferred date & time (subject to courier’s availability) or you may drop off the parcel at courier branch to avoid any delay in processing your shipment.

title: Can I modify or cancel a delivery?

parentDocument.title: Can I modify or cancel a delivery?
parentDocument.content:
Can I modify or cancel a delivery?
You cannot modify your orderonce payment has been confirmed and a tracking number has been generated. However, you can cancel and create a new one. For cancellation, you can do so if the status is ‘Order Created’. If the status of the shipment has changed to ‘Ready’, you will need to contact our Customer Service for a cancellation request with the reason for cancellation. Refund will be credited back if your parcel isconfirmed not being picked up by courier/dropped off at the courier branch. In cases where the canceled shipment was sent to the receiver, we will deduct your credit back.

title: Understanding Return to Sender (RTS) and How to Prevent It

parentDocument.title: Understanding Return to Sender (RTS) and How to Prevent It
parentDocument.content:
6. Shipping tips & tricks
Understanding Return to Sender (RTS) and How to Prevent It
What is Return to Sender (RTS)?
Return to Sender (RTS) occurs when a package is sent back to the original shipper due to issues that prevent successful delivery to the intended recipient. Common reasons for RTS include incorrect or incomplete delivery addresses, incorrect or contact no. not reachable, the consignee being unavailable to receive the package, or the package being refused by the consignee.
For businesses, RTS can lead to increased costs, delayed deliveries, and dissatisfied customers. For consumers, it may mean waiting longer for your products or missing out on important deliveries.
How to Avoid RTS from Happening
To minimize the chances of a package being returned to the sender, consider the following best practices:
Ensure Accurate and Complete Address and Contact InformationDouble-check that the delivery address is accurate and complete before shipping. Include all necessary details, such as apartment numbers, street names, postal codes, and any specific instructions for hard-to-find locations. Make sure the consignee’s contact no. is correct and active.
Double-check that the delivery address is accurate and complete before shipping. Include all necessary details, such as apartment numbers, street names, postal codes, and any specific instructions for hard-to-find locations. Make sure the consignee’s contact no. is correct and active.
Communicate with the ConsigneeKeep the consignee informed about the delivery progress. Providing tracking information and delivery updates can help ensure someone is available to receive the package.
Keep the consignee informed about the delivery progress. Providing tracking information and delivery updates can help ensure someone is available to receive the package.
Use Reliable Shipping PartnersChoose a reliable shipping provider that offers comprehensive tracking services and multiple delivery attempts. A good courier will also provide options for re-attemps or pick-up if the first attempt is unsuccessful.
Choose a reliable shipping provider that offers comprehensive tracking services and multiple delivery attempts. A good courier will also provide options for re-attemps or pick-up if the first attempt is unsuccessful.
Address Potential Delivery IssuesIf you’re aware of potential delivery issues (e.g., consignee is out of town), consider delaying the shipment or arranging for it to be held until the consignee is available.
If you’re aware of potential delivery issues (e.g., consignee is out of town), consider delaying the shipment or arranging for it to be held until the consignee is available.
Best Practices for Re-Delivery After RTS
If a package has been returned to the sender, following best practices for re-delivery can help ensure a smooth and successful process:
Inspect the Returned PackageBefore attempting re-delivery, inspect the returned package for any signs of damage. If damage is found, assess whether the product is still suitable for re-delivery or if it needs to be replaced.
Before attempting re-delivery, inspect the returned package for any signs of damage. If damage is found, assess whether the product is still suitable for re-delivery or if it needs to be replaced.
Contact the ConsigneeReach out to the consignee to confirm the correct delivery address and contact no. Address any issues that led to the RTS to prevent it from happening again.
Reach out to the consignee to confirm the correct delivery address and contact no. Address any issues that led to the RTS to prevent it from happening again.
Update Address InformationIf the RTS was due to an incorrect address or contact no., update the address information in your ordering system before re-sending the package.
If the RTS was due to an incorrect address or contact no., update the address information in your ordering system before re-sending the package.
Monitor the Re-Delivery ProcessTrack the re-delivery closely to ensure that it proceeds smoothly. Notify the consignee of the package’s progress and confirm successful delivery once it’s completed.
Track the re-delivery closely to ensure that it proceeds smoothly. Notify the consignee of the package’s progress and confirm successful delivery once it’s completed.
Return to Sender (RTS) can be a costly and frustrating experience for both shippers and consignees. By understanding the common causes of RTS and implementing preventative measures, you can reduce the likelihood of packages being returned. In cases where RTS does occur, following best practices for re-delivery ensures a higher success rate for subsequent deliveries, enhancing customer satisfaction and reducing operational costs.
By taking these proactive steps, you can create a more efficient and reliable delivery process that benefits everyone involved.

title: FAQ for Return to Sender (RTS) Process

parentDocument.title: FAQ for Return to Sender (RTS) Process
parentDocument.content:
6. Shipping tips & tricks
FAQ for Return to Sender (RTS) Process
Q1: What does RTS (Return to Sender) mean?A:RTS occurs when a package is returned to the shipper due to issues such as incorrect address, incorrect phone no. or consignee not reachable, refusal by the consignee, or failure to collect the package within the stipulated time.
Q2: Why would a package be returned to the sender?A:Common reasons include:
Incorrect or incomplete delivery address.
Incorrect or consignee’s contact no. not reachable.
The consignee was not available to receive the package.
The consignee refused to accept the package.
The package was not collected from a pick-up point within the specified timeframe.
Q3: How will I be notified if a package is being returned?A:You will receive a notification via email, depending on your account settings, informing you that the package is being returned and the reason for the return.
Q4: Will I be charged for RTS?A:Yes, there may be additional charges for returning the package, depending on the shipping policy of the service provider. Some of the service provider didn’t charge for RTS within West Malaysia but if RTS between West Malaysia and East Malaysia normal charges may applied. These charges typically cover the cost of returning the item to you.
Q5: What should I do when I receive an RTS package?A:Inspect the package to determine if there’s any damage. You can then decide to re-ship the item, contact the consignee for clarification, or process a refund.
Q6: Can I stop an RTS package from being returned?A:If you realize an issue before the package is returned, you may contact the courier to attempt a re-delivery or hold the package. However, this is not always guaranteed and depends on the courier’s policy.
Q7: What happens if the package is damaged upon return?A:If the package is damaged during the return process, you may file a claim with the courier service. The outcome will depend on the shipping insurance and the terms and conditions of the courier. You may contact our Live Chat customer support for claim processing.
Q1: What does RTS (Return to Sender) mean?A:RTS occurs when a package you were supposed to receive is returned to the shipper due to delivery issues, such as an incorrect address, incorrect contact no. or the contact no. is not reachable, or your refusal to accept the package.
Q2: Why was my package returned to the sender?A:Common reasons include:
Incorrect or incomplete delivery address.
Incorrect or contact no. not reachable.
You were unavailable to receive the package.
You refused to accept the package.
The package was not collected from a pick-up point within the specified timeframe.
Q3: How can I avoid having my package returned to the sender?A:Ensure that your delivery address and contact no. is accurate and complete. If you miss a delivery attempt, arrange for re-delivery or pick up the package from the designated location as soon as possible.
Q4: What should I do if I want to receive the package that was returned to the sender?A:Contact the shipper as soon as possible to arrange for the package to be re-sent. Depending on the situation, there may be additional shipping charges.
Q5: Can I request a re-delivery before the package is returned to the sender?A:Yes, if you missed a delivery attempt, you can often arrange a re-delivery through the courier service before the package is returned to the sender. You may contact our Live Chat customer support for assistance.
Q6: What happens if I refuse to accept the package?A:If you refuse the package, it will be returned to the sender. Depending on the shipper’s policy, you may be eligible for a refund or the package may be re-sent after resolving any issues.
Q7: Will I be charged for an RTS?A:Charges for RTS are typically borne by the shipper, but if you request a re-delivery after the package has been returned, you may be responsible for the new shipping fees.

title: Items Guidelines

parentDocument.title: Items Guidelines
parentDocument.content:
6. Shipping tips & tricks
Make sure to follow the guidelines below to ensure your package when it will get gets picked up on your pickup day. If these aren’t followed, your package will behold or reject by courier partners.
Below are the Dangerous Good which our courier partners DO NOT PICK UP:
Explosive (Eg: Munitions, Fireworks)
Flammable / Toxic Gas (Eg: Oxygen, Lighter, Aerosols)
Flammable Liquid (Eg: Petrol, Turpentine, heating Fuels)
Flammable Solid (Eg: Matches, Sulphur, Metals Powders)
Oxidising (Eg: Matches, Sulphur, Metals Powders)
Toxic (Eg: Cyanide, Clinical wastes, Biological Samples)
Radioactive (Eg: Uranium, Medical Equipment)
Corrosive (Eg: Acids, Alkalis)
Miscellanous (Eg: Dry Ice, Engines, Lithium ion batteries)
Meanwhile, Below are theRESTRICTED ITEMCannot be Sent:
Pornographic Materials
Original Official Document such as (Birth Certificate, Identity Card, Passport etc)
IATA Restricted Items
Human remains / Organ

title: What is the cut-off booking time for pick-up service?

parentDocument.title: What is the cut-off booking time for pick-up service?
parentDocument.content:
What is the cut-off booking time for pick-up service?
To ensure parcel is pick up on the same day, you will need to book for shipment before 11 am. Any orders that were made after 11 am, will be picked up on the next working day.

title: Where to get Poslaju PL9 Form

parentDocument.title: Where to get Poslaju PL9 Form
parentDocument.content:
Where to get Poslaju PL9 Form
PL9 form is a form that needs to be attached with our parcel when you want to send the goods to Pos Office or Pos Laju. Some Poslaju required this PL9 form and some is not.  Some of us still are not aware of how to get this form. If you’re a first time, you can get this form from the counter of Post Office or Poslaju. If you need more of this, you can ask the staff from the counter.
As shown above, this form containaccount noand oldaccount no.You just need to fill the account no with the account no you can find from the consignment notes below. While thestart serial no and end serial nois the tracking no from the consignment notes. One PL9 form can fit 6 tracking no in one form.

title: How to deliver motorcycles?

parentDocument.title: How to deliver motorcycles?
parentDocument.content:
How to deliver motorcycles?
Log in to your account
1. On your dashboard, click New Order > Single Order
2. Fill in the sender and receiver details at 1. ‘Sender Details’ & ‘2. Receiver Details’
3. For the item type, choose MOTORCYCLES and put your motorcycle CC in the Item Type section. For example 500
4. Please fill in your motorcycle details in the Notes section for courier reference
5. Select the date & time your motorcycle is ready to be picked up
6. Choose ‘POSMOTO’ as the courier service provider
7. Choose any preferred add-ons services (optional)
Helmet Delivery +RM15
Motorcycle Wrapping +RM20
Insurance +RM1.06 or 1% from motorcycle value, whichever is higher
8. Choose Credit Balance as your Payment Method
9. The system will generate a consignment note label if your order is successful
Kindly print out the consignment note label and prepare the owner’s IC copy, grant copy, cover not copy (if applicable) & any supporting documents. These documents need to be given to the driver upon pick up.

title: What should I do if my parcel is lost/damaged?

parentDocument.title: What should I do if my parcel is lost/damaged?
parentDocument.content:
What should I do if my parcel is lost/damaged?
Customers can file for lost/damaged compensation claims from DelyvaNow platform if the parcel is being lost/damaged by the DelyvaNow courier provider. You can contact us via Live Chat or email us at[email protected]. Please submit the tracking number, description of the claim, and photos. Kindly note claim needs to be made within 3 days from the date of delivery.
DelyvaNow has the right to reject or dismiss any claim submission that does not adhere to claimguidelinesor more than 3 days from the date of delivery. Claims will be done on a case-by-case basis. Contact our customer support for more help. Claim cases can take up to 1-3months to be processed.
a. Lost parcel claims process
Ensure the parceldoes not exceed 30 days from the date of its first tracking status being updated.
Customers must be able to provide an airway bill or PL9 form (optional) if the order is lost before there is any tracking status being updated.
Send your proof of delivery and order details to us through email at[email protected]or to our customer service team.
b. Damaged parcel claims process
Provide supporting documents such as air waybill and image of damaged parcel.
Send the supporting documents through email or to our customer service team.
Please take note that certain DelyvaNow Logistics Partners do not allow compensation claim for certain nature of product. For sellers that sell such item, please do ensure you have packaged the item safely to minimise the risk of being damaged in transit.
Non claimable item would beLiquid, Fragile, Poor Packaging and Prohibited item.

title: Goods in transit insurance

parentDocument.title: Goods in transit insurance
parentDocument.content:
Goods in transit insurance
This coverage is against loss of or damage to the goods by theft or accidental means (except as provided in the exclusion) whilst in transit:
in or on or being loaded on or unloaded from any road vehicle.
or whilst temporary housed in the ordinary course of transit whether on or off the said conveyance within the territorial limit.
On lawful General Cargo (non-hazardous) and / or no fragile nature inclusive consumer goods, general industry machinery and equipment, except the following goods category :
Antiques / work of arts, all valuable items such as jewellery, precious metals, precious stones, currency, stamps, coins and similar kind.
Highly attractive / target goods – computer, mobile phone, smart phone, iPad / tablet, valuable watches and cameras, valuable perfumes.
Livestock and / or bloodstock.
Highly breakable / fragile – Chinaware, crystals, earthenware and glassware.
Dangerous, explosive or inflammable nature cargo.
Temperature control goods / refer cargo.
Shipment within Malaysia
Exclude rusting, Oxidization, Discoloration, Denting, Twisting, Bending and Scratching unless caused by insured perils
Exclude mlectrical or Mechanical derangement unless caused by insured perils
Exclude mysterious disappearance, Unexplained loss in weight/wear, Tear, Moth, Vermin, inherent vice, Gradual deterioration or loss due to faulty nature
Exclude pre-existing damages
Excess Terms (Deduction amount payable)
RM50.00 on each and every lossExample: Item value RM600.00, Amount payable by insurance company is RM550.00 after deduction.
Between RM1,000.01 – RM10,000.00
RM250.00 on each and every lossExample: Item value of RM1,000.00, Amount payable by insurance company is RM750.00 after deduction.
Frequently Ask Questions
Q: How much is the insurance premium and what is the limit of liability?A: Insurance premium is 1% from goods cost value or minimum of RM1.00 with SST 6% apply and the limit of liability is RM10,000.00 per shipment.
A: Insurance premium is 1% from goods cost value or minimum of RM1.00 with SST 6% apply and the limit of liability is RM10,000.00 per shipment.
Q: Is the additional insurance coverage available for all delivery services?A: No. The additional insurance coverage only applicable for instant delivery, domestic delivery & international delivery within southeast Asia only.
A: No. The additional insurance coverage only applicable for instant delivery, domestic delivery & international delivery within southeast Asia only.
Q: How much is the insurance premium?A: Any product value below RM400 will be covered at premium of RM1.00 per consignment note. Any product value above RM400 will be covered at premium of 1% from goods invoice value. SST 6% apply.
A: Any product value below RM400 will be covered at premium of RM1.00 per consignment note. Any product value above RM400 will be covered at premium of 1% from goods invoice value. SST 6% apply.
Q: What is the limit of liability?A: RM10,000.00 per shipment.
A: RM10,000.00 per shipment.
Q: Can I purchase the insurance coverage after shipment booking?A: No. You need to purchase insurance coverage during booking by select addons insurance coverage in the booking forms.
A: No. You need to purchase insurance coverage during booking by select addons insurance coverage in the booking forms.
Q: Do I need to declare description of the goods?A: Yes. You need to give specific goods description such as brand, specification of the goods, colour and etc.
A: Yes. You need to give specific goods description such as brand, specification of the goods, colour and etc.
Q: When will I receive the cover note?A: We will process your insurance and send copy of cover note to your registered email immediately after booking confirmation.
A: We will process your insurance and send copy of cover note to your registered email immediately after booking confirmation.
Q: I cancel my shipment, can I cancel and get refund for the purchased insurance?A: Yes you may cancel your purchased insurance before the delivery commence. We will process the refund and transfer to your bank account within 14 working days.
A: Yes you may cancel your purchased insurance before the delivery commence. We will process the refund and transfer to your bank account within 14 working days.
Q: What should I do in the event of loss or damage?A: You must notify us thru email with full details of the incident / accident within 48 hours from information received or goods received. Early notification is required to avoid any prejudice to your claim. If theft and / or injury to third party, make a police report immediately.
A: You must notify us thru email with full details of the incident / accident within 48 hours from information received or goods received. Early notification is required to avoid any prejudice to your claim. If theft and / or injury to third party, make a police report immediately.
Q: How to submit a claim?A: You must submit your claim with all supporting information and documents as requested to us. Documents include photocopy of IC or passport, copy of invoice of the goods, copy of consignment note, copy of cover note, picture for damage cases and police report for theft cases. You will requires to give full cooperation to the surveyors / adjusters appointed by us in assessing your claim. Further documents may be asked by the insurance provider to validate the claim.
A: You must submit your claim with all supporting information and documents as requested to us. Documents include photocopy of IC or passport, copy of invoice of the goods, copy of consignment note, copy of cover note, picture for damage cases and police report for theft cases. You will requires to give full cooperation to the surveyors / adjusters appointed by us in assessing your claim. Further documents may be asked by the insurance provider to validate the claim.
Q: When will I receive the claim reimbursement?A: Upon receiving your claims documents, you will receive a letter of offer or rejection it’s does not fall under policy condition from the insurance company within 3 working weeks. Upon agreement, payment will be made within 1 working week.
A: Upon receiving your claims documents, you will receive a letter of offer or rejection it’s does not fall under policy condition from the insurance company within 3 working weeks. Upon agreement, payment will be made within 1 working week.
Q: What is excess means?A: Excess is the amount paid by you and this amount will be deducted from the final amount payable from the insurance company if the claim falls under the policy condition. (Refer example on Excess for a sample of calculation).
A: Excess is the amount paid by you and this amount will be deducted from the final amount payable from the insurance company if the claim falls under the policy condition. (Refer example on Excess for a sample of calculation).
Q: Who can I contact to check on the claim status?A: You may reach us at Live Chat or email to[email protected]. You also can contact Senang Malaysia via e-mail[email protected]or call +6011-1180 9931.
A: You may reach us at Live Chat or email to[email protected]. You also can contact Senang Malaysia via e-mail[email protected]or call +6011-1180 9931.

title: How do I file an insurance claim?

parentDocument.title: How do I file an insurance claim?
parentDocument.content:
How do I file an insurance claim?
Contact us at[email protected]with Subject: Insurance Claim – Tracking Number / Cover Note Number. Please submit the tracking number, description of the claim, photo of the damaged parcel (if applicable) & copy of the cover note. Kindly note claim needs to be made within 14 days from the last date of shipment last updated.
DelyvaNow has the right to reject or dismiss any claim submission that does not adhere to claim guidelines or more than 14 days from the date of shipment last updated.
An insurance claim will be submitted to Senangpks via Delyva.
The claim will be done on a case-by-case basis. Contact our customer support for more help.

title: How to purchase insurance coverage?

parentDocument.title: How to purchase insurance coverage?
parentDocument.content:
How to purchase insurance coverage?
1. Indicate accurately the item invoice value with item description as details as possible (brand, specification, type, etc.) in the Item Page.
2. Tick Insurance box in Add Ons section and key in the value of the item. Please ensure item value is properly declared as this affects the sum of value insured.

title: Are my shipments insured?

parentDocument.title: Are my shipments insured?
parentDocument.content:
Are my shipments insured?
We at DelyvaNow understand your concern for the safety of your packages and how important it is to have peace of mind.
We offer liability coverage of up to RM300 for any packages that have been picked up/collected by our riders that are damaged or lost(depending on the courier service you selected)
You may also purchase additional insurance premiums for as low as RM1.06. Learn more on how to purchase insurance.

title: How can I add courier service at my account?

parentDocument.title: How can I add courier service at my account?
parentDocument.content:
How can I add courier service at my account?
You can add it by using the steps below:
Go your account and click “Settings”.
Click on “Service Providers”.
Add your preferred courier(s).

title: Developer Guide – Integrating with Delyva API

parentDocument.title: Developer Guide – Integrating with Delyva API
parentDocument.content:
Developer Guide – Integrating with Delyva API
Overview:This guide explains how to integrate with Delyva’s delivery API across two models (Store/Marketplace and SaaS/Platform) and covers the key endpoints to quote services, create/process orders, print labels, track deliveries, embed a live map with ETA, and receive webhooks. Examples use curl, Node.js, and PHP. .
Recommended Settings for SaaS/Platform
Base URL & Authentication
Process / Confirm Order
Embeddable Live Map with ETA
Add-Ons: COD & Insurance
Sandbox vs Production
Postman Collection Snippet
1) Integration Models
One Delyva account managed by the system owner.
All merchants use the owner’s account (shared credentials).
Billing is direct between system owner and Delyva.
Each merchant/subscriber has their own Delyva account and credentials.
Billing is direct between each merchant and Delyva.
Revenue share: SaaS platform earns 1% of order amount (handled by Delyva).
2) Recommended Settings for SaaS/Platform
Create a settings screen for each merchant to store their Delyva configuration:
API ParametersCompany code — textUser ID — textCustomer ID — text/numberItem Type — select (PARCEL/PACKAGE/BULKY)
Customer ID — text/number
Item Type — select (PARCEL/PACKAGE/BULKY)
Checkout RatesEnable/Disable — checkboxCheckout Rates AdjustmentAdjustment type — select: Mark-up / DiscountPercentage rate — number (0–100)Flat rate — number (0–100)Formula: Delivery rate = service price + (% of service price) + flat rateFree shipping threshold — number (0 = disable)
Enable/Disable — checkbox
Checkout Rates AdjustmentAdjustment type — select: Mark-up / DiscountPercentage rate — number (0–100)Flat rate — number (0–100)Formula: Delivery rate = service price + (% of service price) + flat rate
Adjustment type — select: Mark-up / Discount
Percentage rate — number (0–100)
Flat rate — number (0–100)
Formula: Delivery rate = service price + (% of service price) + flat rate
Free shipping threshold — number (0 = disable)
3) Base URL & Authentication
Base URL:https://api.delyva.app/v1.0
Headers (all requests):
Get your API Key under Customer Portal → Settings → API Integrations. For webhook verification, retrieve apiSecret from GET /user → user.apiSecret.
Create/locate your API Key in the portal.
(Optional) Get apiSecret via GET /user.
Create Order with process: false (draft).
Process the draft when shipment is ready.
Get Order Details to read consignmentNo.
Subscribe to Webhooks for status updates.
Embed Live Map with ETA (only for hyperlocal delivery – eg: Instant delivery, same-day delivery, or own fleet delivery).
5) Price Quote (Get services & rates)
Example Response (truncated)
Tip: Save services[i].serviceCompany.companyCode to use when creating the order.
6) Create Order (Draft)
Example Response (truncated)
Save the id for processing, label printing, and tracking.
7) Process / Confirm Order
Example Response (truncated)
Courier (NDD): cancel before statusCode 110 or contact support after label printed.
Instant/Same-Day: cancel before statusCode 200 (after driver accepts).
You can use webhook subscriptions to receive notifications about particular events. After you’ve subscribed to a webhook, you can let your app execute code immediately after specific events occur, instead of having to make API calls periodically to check their status. For example, you can rely on webhooks to trigger an action in your app when order tracking has been updated, or when an order is updated. By using webhook subscriptions you can make fewer API calls overall, which makes sure that your apps are more efficient and update quickly.
After you configure a webhook subscription, the events that you specified will trigger a webhook notification each time they occur. This notification contains a JSON payload, and HTTP headers that provide context. For example, theorder.createdwebhook includes the following headers:
Your application needs to respond with200 (Success)within 30 seconds or it will assume the request has failed and will retry POSTing again several times an hour. DelyvaX will stop trying after 10 failed attempts.
Webhooks are verified by calculating a digital signature. Each webhook request includes a base64-encoded X-Delyvax-Hmac-SHA256 header, which is generated using the user’sapiSecret(user.apiSecret) along with the data sent in the request.
To verify that the request came from DelyvaX, compute the HMAC digest according to the following algorithm and compare it to the value in the X-Delyvax-Hmac-SHA256 header. If they match, then you can be sure that the webhook was sent from DelyvaX.
List of Supported Webhook Events
Example payload (order_tracking.update)
Simulate Webhook Updates (Testing)
For sandbox testing, you can simulate webhook events against your own test orders without waiting for a real courier status update.
Use the Delyva Sandbox Webhook Simulator:
https://dx-integration-sandbox.pages.dev/
Enter your testconsingmentNoortrackingNo.
Select the event (e.g. Pickup Success, Out for Delivery, Delivered).
Send the simulated event — it will POST a webhook payload to your subscribed endpoint.
Verify your system receives and processes the update as expected.
This is useful for end-to-end testing of webhook handling before going live.
12) Embeddable Live Map with ETA
On the way to pick up — after driver is accepted/assigned and before pickup is completed.
On the way to deliver — after pickup is completed and before delivered/failed/cancelled.
Branding: Custom pin icons/branding available for DelyvaNow+ and DelyvaX subscribers.
13) Add-Ons: COD & Insurance
Quote with add-ons (re-quote specific service to get adjusted price):
Create order with add-ons:
15) Common Service Types
NDD — Next Day Delivery (pickup)
NDD-DROP — Next Day Delivery (drop-off)
COD-NDD — NDD with COD (pickup)
COD-NDD-DROP — NDD with COD (drop-off)
SD — Same day (~3–4 hours)
MOTO — Motorcycle transport
16) Sandbox vs Production
API Base: https://api.delyva.app/v1.0
Production Portal: https://my.delyva.app/customer
Sandbox/Dev Portal: https://trydx.delyva.app/customer
CDN (labels/assets): https://cdn.delyva.app
17) Postman Collection Snippet (Import)

title: Cold Chain Packaging guidelines – Ninja Van

parentDocument.title: Cold Chain Packaging guidelines – Ninja Van
parentDocument.content:
Cold Chain Packaging guidelines – Ninja Van
Example product and packaging guidelines

title: FAQ Cold Chain Delivery Service – Ninja Van

parentDocument.title: FAQ Cold Chain Delivery Service – Ninja Van
parentDocument.content:
FAQ Cold Chain Delivery Service – Ninja Van
Q: What is temperature control option that available?
A:We understand that different products require specific temperature ranges to maintain their freshness, quality, and safety. Here’s a breakdown of our temperature control options:
Temperature control (18°C to 25°C). This range ideal for products that require moderate chilling but shouldn’t freeze.
Chilled (0°C to 8°C). Perfect for preserving the freshness and quality of fruits, vegetables, dairy products and fresh meat.
Frozen (-18°C to -20°C). This ultra-cold environment is essential for frozen goods like seafood, meats, prepared meals, and ice cream.
Q: What are the packaging guidelines for cold chain shipments with Ninja Van Cold Chain service?
A: General packaging guidelines:
Packaging must be sealed: Ensure your package is securely sealed to prevent leakage or contamination.
Suitable box materials: We recommend using sturdy cardboard boxes, styrofoam boxes or insulated foam packs for optimal temperature control.
Max weight and dimensions: Packages cannot exceed 25kg in weight and 50cm x 63cm x 38cm in dimensions.
Minimize empty space: Use void fill material like packing peanuts foam, bubble wrap or foam inserts to minimize empty space within the box. This helps to maintain consistent temperatures and prevents items from shifting during transport.
Q: Do I need a styrofoam box?
A:No, a sturdy cardboard box is fine if your items are already chilled/frozen at pick-up. For extra insulation, consider a styrofoam box. Refer to your product’s packaging.
Q: Who are the target segments for this service?
A:Ninja Van Cold Chain offers temperature-controlled logistics solutions for a variety of industries:
Fresh & Frozen Segment: We cater to food suppliers, manufacturers, wholesalers, distributors, retailers, grocery chains, restaurants, and e-commerce businesses dealing with fresh and frozen food items.
Temp Control Segment: Our services are also suitable for pharmaceutical suppliers, distributors, retailers, clinics, and hospitals that require precise temperature control for medication and other temperature-sensitive healthcare products. We also serve Food and Beverage businesses for temperature-sensitive items like chocolate and other products requiring a controlled environment.
Q: When can I expect my delivery?
A:We understand the importance of timely deliveries. Here is the breakdown of our typical delivery timeframes:
KV to KV: You can expect your shipment to arrive the next day within Klang Valley.
KV to Outstation and vice versa: Deliveries between Klang Valley and Outstation usually take additional one business day, ensuring freshness for your temperature-sensitive goods.
Q: Does Ninja Van Cold Chain deliver cold items in cars and motorcycles?
A:No, we uses refrigerated trucks and vans with controlled temperatures for all cold items, ensuring an unbroken cold chain for freshness.
Q: What types of vehicles does Ninja Van Cold Chain use?
A:We utilise a diverse fleet specifically designed for optimal cold chain logistics. This includes:
10 ft and 14 ft reefer vehicles: Ideal for smaller shipments requiring precise temperature control.
Multi-temperature zone vehicles (coming soon): We’re planning to introduce innovative vehicles that can handle multiple temperature zones within a single container, maximising efficiency for your cold chain needs.
Temperature-controlled light goods vehicles (coming soon): Perfect for expedited deliveries of smaller temperature-sensitive items.
Ready to enhance your cold chain logistics?
Sign up nowor contact our Sales team to learn how Ninja Van Cold Chain services can support your business needs!

title: How Cold Chain Delivery Works?

parentDocument.content:
How Cold Chain Delivery Works?
Who Can Benefit from Cold Chain Delivery?This service is perfect for businesses and industries such as:
Food & Beverage:Deliver fresh produce, dairy, meat, seafood, frozen meals, and other perishables.
Pharmaceuticals:Transport vaccines, insulin, and other temperature-sensitive medications.

How Cold Chain Delivery Works
Temperature Monitoring: The cold chain process includes real-time temperature tracking to ensure that the required temperature is maintained throughout the delivery process.
Specialized Packaging: Products are packed in insulated boxes or temperature-controlled containers to help retain their temperature during transit.
Designated Vehicles: Dedicated refrigerated vehicles equipped with the necessary equipment transport the goods while ensuring they remain within the desired temperature range.
Benefits of  Cold Chain Delivery with DelyvaNow:

Wide Coverage: Available for chilled and frozen deliveries across various regions.
Real-Time Tracking: Monitor your shipments in real-time, ensuring transparency and peace of mind.
Temperature-Specific Handling: Products are handled with care, ensuring the right temperature conditions are met from pick-up to delivery.

title: Why Cold Chain Delivery is Important?

parentDocument.title: Why Cold Chain Delivery is Important?
parentDocument.content:
Why Cold Chain Delivery is Important?
Cold Chain Delivery plays a vital role in maintaining the integrity of temperature-sensitive goods. Without proper temperature control, perishable items can spoil, lose effectiveness, or become unsafe for use. Cold Chain Delivery helps businesses:
Preserve product freshness.
Ensure compliance with health and safety regulations.
Minimize product waste due to spoilage or contamination.
Build customer trust by delivering high-quality products.

title: What is Cold Chain Delivery?

parentDocument.title: What is Cold Chain Delivery?
parentDocument.content:
What is Cold Chain Delivery?
Cold Chain Delivery refers to a temperature-controlled logistics process used to transport perishable products such as food, pharmaceuticals, and other sensitive items that require specific temperature ranges to maintain quality and safety. The “cold chain” ensures that these products are stored, handled, and transported in an environment that keeps them chilled or frozen, depending on their requirements.

Normally it will take around 7-10 days depending on the courier booked and the parcel destination. Transit will take around 4-5 working days subject to customs.

title: HS Code

parentDocument.title: HS Code
parentDocument.content:
International Shipping
What is HS code (Harmonized System Code)?
The Harmonized System (HS) code is a categorization system created, developed, and maintained by the World Customs Organization (WCO). Every commodity is tagged to an HS code, and the code assigned to it is internationally recognized in almost every country and is commonly used in customs to clear shipments.
How to set HS Code at DelyvaNow Customer Portal?
While placing an order with DelyvaNow Customer portal, all you need to do is fill in the item type on HS Code form, and our system will automatically populate the HS Code for you.
So you do not need to engage with a customs broker in order to determine the correct HS Code for your item.
What happens if I use the incorrect HS code?
Tariff classifications can be open to interpretation, which can result in a shipment being misclassified. Regardless of the reason, misclassified shipments can have multiple adverse consequences including:
Overpayment/Underpayment of duties
Failure to utilize free trade agreements or used in error
Lead fines, liquidation and other penalties
Unexpected customs clearance delays
Seizure of the products
Denial of import/export privileges
What is the purpose of HS code in import and export?
HS codes are used to classify and define goods traded internationally to determine rate of duty, eligibility for exemptions, qualification for approved manufacturer/assembler tariff provisions and calculation of any other additional taxes (e.g. excise).
If you have any questions or concerns, please don’t hesitate to contact us via Live Chat from  8 AM – 10 PM (GMT +8).
